title: alchemilla pratensis
intro: Alchemilla pratensis, also known as the lady's mantle or common lady's mantle, is a perennial flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and protect wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The plant's medicinal properties include astringent and hemostatic effects, making it useful for treating menstrual disorders, heavy bleeding, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Alchemilla pratensis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to ease menstrual cramps, promote wound healing, and support overall digestive health.
